channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The first sunlight reflecting space mirror has been cleared for launch
item.description: Reflect Orbital has been given the green light to launch its first space mirror that aims to redirect sunlight down to Earth at night. The US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) has authorized the California-based startup to build and operate a single prototype satellite in low-Earth orbit later this year, despite concerns over how the technology […]...

item.title: Oura Ring 5 Review: Still the Smart Ring to Beat
item.description: It’s not the upgrade Ring 4 owners have been waiting for, but it’s easily the best smart ring Oura has ever made.

item.title: 'You essentially pay for intelligence twice, once with money, and again with something even more valuable': Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella warns AI users not to give away too much
item.description: By moving to on-prem models trained on the data businesses already keep in the cloud, their is less risk of the big AI companies selling business secrets to the competition.

item.title: Volkswagen veut fermer quatre usines, dont son temple de l’électrique
item.description: Jusqu'à 100 000 postes supprimés et quatre usines rayées de la carte, dont Zwickau, berceau de ses voitures électriques. Le plan de Volkswagen est si brutal que son propre conseil de surveillance l'a recalé.

item.title: This German Man Is on a Quest to Cut the Perfect Slice of Bread
item.description: Armed with high-end knives and digital calipers, Germanbreadcutter has entranced thousands of fans, one loaf at a time.

item.title: Let’s build a children’s public internet
item.description: An increasing number of people seem to agree the internet is terrible for children - allegedly addictive, destructive to self-esteem, possibly a portal to predators. Over the past year, several countries have started requiring stringent age verification or outright bans for minors. At the end of June in the US, the House of Representatives passed […]...

item.title: ‘Those non-pro colors are incredible:’ the Google Pixel 11 series has leaked on Amazon, and the colors are going down better than the specs
item.description: The Google Pixel 11 series might be sold in some eye-catching colors, but the specs could be a real disappointment.

item.title: Inference needs memory: how context is becoming AI infrastructure
item.description: As enterprise AI becomes more complex, AI architectures can no longer treat context as temporary.

item.title: New York enacts first US ban on data center construction
item.description: New York has become the first US state to place a moratorium on the construction of data centers.

item.title: The AI job apocalypse is a myth. We need more human talent than ever before
item.description: Outlining why the AI jobs apocalypse isn't happening.

item.title: X's algorithm will finally prioritize replies from people you already follow
item.description: X has made a tweak to give your replies a boost in your friends' comment section.

item.title: This Luddite Puppet Hopes You’re Not Reading This on Your Smartphone
item.description: On this week’s episode of The Big Interview podcast, WIRED’s senior culture editor Manisha Krishnan talks to Gowanus about eschewing Big Tech, going outside, and rejection in the age of dating apps.

item.title: France-Espagne : comment voir la demi-finale de la Coupe du monde 2026 en streaming ?
item.description: La France affronte l'Espagne en demi-finale de la Coupe du monde 2026, ce mardi 14 juillet à 21h00 (heure française). M6 diffuse le match gratuitement, et beIN Sports 1 le retransmet également, voici tout ce qu'il faut savoir pour ne rien rater.

item.title: On a trouvé le clone de l’Apple Watch Ultra et il est soldé à -46 % sur Amazon
item.description: La Huawei Watch Fit 4 Pro est une montre connectée sportive en titane disponible pour 149 euros au lieu de 279 euros sur Amazon. Une belle remise de 130 euros qui la rend difficile à ignorer, même face à l'arrivée de sa remplaçante.

item.title: Five reasons switching from IP VPN to SD WAN will help you build an AI-ready network
item.description: The scale, speed and complexity of modern cloud and AI workloads demand SD WAN.

item.title: Fin des jeux physiques en 2028 : Sony est attaqué en justice dans deux pays
item.description: L'abandon des jeux physiques ne passe pas. Quelques jours après l'annonce de Sony, deux plaintes visent déjà l'entreprise aux Pays-Bas et au Mexique. Le reproche est le même.

item.title: 'It's exploding now?' — Reddit user's Zotac RTX 5090 'explodes' while playing Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced, and I'm starting to get worried that high-powered GPUs aren't safe
item.description: Nvidia RTX 5090 GPU melting issues aren't a big surprise anymore, but this latest case may be the worst of them all.

item.title: Britain's AI push is exposing a memory crisis inside business
item.description: AI is exposing a hidden organizational memory crisis that undermines productivity and customer experience.

item.title: Adieu la paperasse ! Voici AMI : une méga-application pour toutes vos démarches administratives en France (et ça serait presque prêt)
item.description: L'État travaille sur une application mobile unique pour regrouper toutes vos démarches en ligne, des impôts à la carte grise. Elle s'appelle AMI et pourrait arriver dès octobre.

item.title: 'AI writing is now a problem everywhere on social media': Study finds nearly half of all LinkedIn long posts are AI-generated, and that's only the start
item.description: Online networks are drowning in AI-written slop, report finds - with LinkedIn the worst hit.

item.title: Tesla verrouille son FSD : la caméra intérieure pourra désormais vous bloquer
item.description: Les caméras embarquées dans l’habitacle des Tesla contrôleront votre identité avant d’utiliser le FSD. C’est en tout cas ce qui se profile après la découverte d’une ligne de code ajoutée dans la dernière mise à jour de l’application iOS de Tesla.

item.title: The Chatbot That Foretold Why People Share Secrets With ChatGPT
item.description: In the 1960s an MIT professor named Joseph Weizenbaum created a chatbot called ELIZA. The conversations people had with it set precedents for the chatbots to come.

item.title: Marvel has reportedly cast a former Star Wars actor as Magneto in its new X-Men movie — and they nearly played The Fantastic Four's Reed Richards in the MCU
item.description: A major star long rumored to play Mr. Fantastic in Marvel's cinematic juggernaut will reportedly play the iconic mutant.

item.title: This designer is using AI to make clothing with zero fabric waste
item.description: Shelly Xu Designs uses AI to reengineer clothing patterns, cutting fabric waste to near zero—without asking factories or consumers to change a thing. Walk onto the cutting floor of almost any garment factory in the world and you’ll see rolls of fabric laid out in stacks. A technician arranges a pattern on the cloth to cut out the building blocks for the garment: sleeves, pockets, bodice....

item.title: Why Democrats are retrofitting their old logos for the Maine Senate race
item.description: Candidates are rushing to replace Graham Platner as the Democratic nominee for Maine’s U.S. Senate seat, leading many to reuse old campaign branding. On July 10, after allegations of sexual assault and mistreatment of women, former nominee Graham Platner formally withdrew from the U.S. Senate race in Maine. To pick a last-minute replacement, Maine Democrats are holding a nominating convention on July 25 in time for a state deadline to put forward a new candidate who will face off against Republican Senator Susan Collins in November. With no time to spare, would-be nominees are retrofitting old...

item.title: The FCC just approved a test of a giant mirror in space
item.description: Scientists warn Reflect Orbital’s plan to beam sunlight back to Earth after dark could disrupt wildlife and human sleep. The Federal Communications Commission has given a California startup permission to launch and test a satellite that would use a giant mirror to reflect sunlight back to Earth after sunset, despite objections from astronomers, wildlife experts and others who say the plan could disrupt scientific research and interfere with the sleep patterns of living organisms....

item.title: No, NordVPN does not drain your phone battery
item.description: Always switching your VPN off to save your charge? New independent testing from West Coast Labs shows that notorious smartphone battery drain is mostly a myth, with NordVPN consuming under 2% of your daily battery life.

item.title: These four skills can help you fulfill your purpose—and anyone can master them
item.description: The surprising power of ‘directed choices.’ In a recent opinion piece for Fast Company, I wrote about five qualities that will distinguish exceptional leaders in the age of artificial intelligence: IQ, EQ, TQ, WQ, and VQ.

item.title: Co-existing with AI: why replacement narratives are holding the public sector back
item.description: Why fears of replacement are preventing public services from embracing AI.

item.title: Google bought all of a major solar farm's output to offset its dirty data center emissions
item.description: Google has struck a deal to purchase a major solar project's entire electrical output to offset its fossil fuel emissions.

item.title: Sotheby’s Big T. Rex Auction Raises Concerns Hype and Wealth Are Upending Science
item.description: Private buyers are increasingly outbidding museums for fossils. That’s making it difficult—or even impossible—for researchers to improve our understanding of the past.
